sections_cited: section 4 of the Sindh Service Tribunals Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT, HYDERABAD C.P. No.D-721 of 2026 [Mst. Kousar Parveen v. Province of Sindh & others] DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) Present: Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry J. Riazat Ali Sahar J. 1. For orders on M.A. No.2962/2026. 2. For orders on office objections. 3. For orders on M.A. No.2963/2026. 4. For orders on M.A. No.2964/2026. 5. For hearing of main case. 21.04.2026 Petitioner present in person. == Adnan Iqbal Chaudhry, J- Urgency granted. Exemption application is granted subject to all just exceptions. The petitioner has challenged order of major penalty, dated 16.12.2022, whereby the petitioner was removed from service as Primary School Teacher in the School Education & Literacy Department. Admittedly, the petitioner was a Civil Servant and the order for removal from service was upheld in departmental appeal. That being so, remedy of the petitioner was before the Service Tribunal under section 4 of the Sindh Service Tribunals Act 1973. The instant petition is therefore barred by Article 212 of the Constitution of Pakistan. For said reason, the petition is dismissed in limine. JUDGE JUDGE Muhammad Danish
